description_long: This is a tall, solitary sunflower with a prominent, round central disk that is deep brown in color, surrounded by a dense ring of bright yellow petals. The petals are arranged radially and overlap slightly, creating a full, symmetrical bloom. The flower is supported by a long, slender green stem that curves gently and is adorned with several broad, ovate green leaves at varying heights. The leaves have visible veins and a matte finish, adding to the naturalistic appearance. The back of the flower head shows green bracts supporting the petals, and the stem appears sturdy yet flexible. This flower would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative roadside vegetation.
description: A tall sunflower with a brown central disk, bright yellow radial petals, and a green curved stem with broad ovate leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the sunflower, with the central disk and symmetrical yellow petals clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the flower's depth, the curvature of the stem, and the layering of petals and leaves.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the green bracts behind the flower head and the arrangement of leaves along the stem.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stem's curve and the profile of the flower head and leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, forming a single whorl around the central disk. They overlap slightly and are symmetrically distributed, typical of the Asteraceae family.
